question: Design a `NotificationSystem` class. Overload its constructor to accept different notification types (e.g., `enum NotificationType { SMS, EMAIL, PUSH }`). Also, overload a `send` method to handle sending messages of different content types (e.g., `std::string`, `int`).

test_cases.input: Execution of the main function, demonstrating correctly implemented `NotificationSystem` with various valid and gracefully handled invalid inputs.
test_cases.expected:
--- Test Case 1: SMS Notification (enum constructor) ---
NotificationSystem initialized for SMS notifications.
Sending message: "Hello from the C++ NotificationSystem!" via SMS. (Length: 38)
Sending code: 101 via SMS. (Code: 101)

--- Test Case 2: EMAIL Notification (enum constructor) ---
NotificationSystem initialized for EMAIL notifications.
Sending message: "Important update: Your monthly report is ready." via EMAIL. (Subject: "Important update: Your monthly report is ready....")
Sending code: 200 via EMAIL. (Error code: 200)

--- Test Case 3: PUSH Notification (enum constructor) ---
NotificationSystem initialized for PUSH notifications.
Sending message: "Your order has been shipped!" via PUSH. (Alert: "Your order has been shipped!")
Sending code: 202 via PUSH. (Status: 202)

--- Test Case 4: SMS Notification (int constructor, valid) ---
NotificationSystem initialized for SMS notifications from integer ID 0.
Sending message: "SMS from integer constructor." via SMS. (Length: 29)
Sending code: 100 via SMS. (Code: 100)

--- Test Case 5: EMAIL Notification (int constructor, valid) ---
NotificationSystem initialized for EMAIL notifications from integer ID 1.
Sending message: "Email from integer constructor." via EMAIL. (Subject: "Email from integer constructor.")
Sending code: 201 via EMAIL. (Error code: 201)

--- Test Case 6: Invalid Type (int constructor, handled gracefully) ---
Warning: Invalid notification type ID (99) provided. Defaulting to SMS notifications.
NotificationSystem initialized for default SMS notifications.
Sending message: "This message should default to SMS." via SMS. (Length: 35)
Sending code: 500 via SMS. (Code: 500)
